runs.markdownReport:
# Daily AI Native Product Radar - 2026-05-14

Manual afternoon backfill for the first day of the automation.

Top themes: AI coding agent management, MCP code intelligence, desktop-control agents, vertical AI copilots.

projects.summary: Open-source AI workspace for teams to build, deploy and manage agents conversationally, visually, or with code.
projects.audience: Teams automating internal workflows across many integrations and model providers.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The product surface is an agent workforce layer: visual/conversational/code authoring, deployment, integrations, model orchestration and team management.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub page describes 1,000+ integrations, every major LLM, Apache-2.0 licensing, docs/community links and an existing project card in the Yuque radar base.
projects.runnability: Repository exposes docs, examples and deployment-oriented structure; current run did not clone locally, so runtime friction still needs direct audit.
projects.recommendedAction: Keep as workflow-builder benchmark; compare against n8n/Activepieces for agent-native collaboration and deployment depth.

projects.summary: Open-source Gemini terminal AI agent for repository-aware coding, shell interaction, and developer assistance.
projects.audience: Developers and teams comparing Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, and other terminal coding agents.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The terminal, repository, file edits, shell commands, and model reasoning become one agentic coding loop.
projects.growthSignal: Surfaced strongly in current code-agent searches with clear CLI product README, auth, and install paths.
projects.runnability: README documents npx/npm installation, authentication, CLI usage examples, and configuration.
projects.recommendedAction: Run locally and compare permissions, context handling, patch review, and tool-call boundaries against Codex CLI.

projects.summary: Long-horizon SuperAgent harness for research, coding, and creation tasks using sandboxes, memories, tools, skills, subagents, and message gateways.
projects.audience: Teams building long-running research, coding, and automation agents.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It treats multi-hour agent execution as a full runtime made of sandboxing, skills, memory, subagents, and messaging channels.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub snapshot showed about 70k stars, 2,186 commits, and product directories for backend, frontend, docker, docs, and skills.
projects.runnability: README documents make docker-start, make up, make dev, localhost:2026, MCP server configuration, and IM channel setup.
projects.recommendedAction: Track as a long-task agent architecture benchmark; audit its Lark/Slack/WeChat task gateways.

projects.skipReason: Mature open coding-agent baseline with strong activity; excluded to preserve today’s focus on newer surrounding product layers.
Important baseline coding agent and currently active, but already known and covered by the user’s actual workflow; not a fresh radar discovery for today.
Repeated abnormal high-star signal from earlier runs; still needs API/community verification before using as a growth datapoint.

projects.summary: Self-hosted AI workspace for chat, agents, deep research, documents, notes/tasks, email/calendar integrations, local/API model backends and admin-gated local tools.
projects.audience: Local-first AI users, builders, creators and small teams who want a private ChatGPT/Claude-style workspace they control.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The product is an AI operating workspace: LLM chat, agent tools, memory, research, documents, calendar/email and model serving converge into one self-hosted app.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend ranked it #2 overall on June 8 with +2.2k stars today and 63.9k stars; GitHub README shows 23 commits, 48 issues, 69 PRs, Docker/native/macOS/Windows launchers and a security-heavy setup guide.
projects.runnability: Docker Compose starts the app plus ChromaDB, SearXNG and ntfy; native macOS/Linux/Windows commands are documented; no local run audit performed.
projects.recommendedAction: High priority trial. Run Docker or macOS launcher locally and inspect agent permissions, admin controls, memory/doc flows and model-provider setup.

projects.summary: Open-source, self-hostable Vapi/Retell alternative for building production voice agents with a drag-and-drop workflow builder.
projects.audience: Teams building customer support, sales, phone automation, or voice-interaction workflows.
projects.aiNativeAngle: LLM/STT/TTS orchestration, BYOK, MCP-native workflows, telephony, and workflow builder are the product core.
projects.growthSignal: Visible GitHub page shows about 4.2k stars, 842 forks, 535 commits, 16 issues, and 12 PRs.
projects.runnability: Has docker-compose files, API, UI, SDK, docs, examples, and product screenshots.
projects.recommendedAction: Run the local Docker stack and study its voice workflow builder architecture.

projects.category: Code Agent Context / MCP Code Intelligence
projects.summary: Local pre-indexed code knowledge graph for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenCode, Gemini, and other coding agents.
projects.audience: Developers and engineering teams that ask coding agents to work inside large repositories.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It directly changes agent context acquisition by exposing repository structure, semantic search, call graphs, and impact analysis through local CLI/MCP tooling.
projects.growthSignal: Continues to appear in recent AI repo growth signals after the 2026-05-28 radar; prior local run recorded about 29.8k stars and a recent v0.9.5 release on 2026-05-25.
projects.runnability: README documents one-line install paths, npx/npm usage, codegraph init, and local operation without API keys.
projects.recommendedAction: Keep as benchmark project; run it against this repo and one larger repo to measure agent tool-call/token reduction.

projects.summary: Open-source product for building persistent AI humans/companions with memory, voice, avatar, and app-like interaction surface.
projects.audience: Builders exploring AI companions, digital humans, coaching/education assistants, and consumer-facing agent experiences.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The product is not a wrapper around chat; identity, persistence, multimodal interaction, and relationship memory are the core product primitives.
projects.growthSignal: Surfaced in a current fastest-growing AI repo roundup with roughly 17k stars and about +15k weekly growth; GitHub page was reachable during the run.
projects.runnability: README/product framing indicates a concrete open-source app surface rather than only a paper or prompt collection; marked for hands-on audit before production use.
projects.recommendedAction: Create a project card and run a local install audit next; compare memory/avatar/voice architecture with consumer companion products.

projects.summary: Terminal-native coding agent for delegating code edits and repo work from the command line.
projects.audience: Software engineers and AI-assisted development teams.
projects.aiNativeAngle: Agentic coding, repo context, tool use, and iterative edits are the core product loop.
projects.growthSignal: OSSInsight AI trending snapshot showed very strong 28-day star growth and high visibility in coding-agent discussions.
projects.runnability: CLI-oriented product surface with install docs and active repo structure.
projects.recommendedAction: Try on one contained repo task and compare edit quality, context handling, and rollback workflow.

projects.category: Local AI App Builder / Vibe Coding Desktop
projects.summary: Local, open-source AI app builder positioned as a v0/Lovable/Bolt alternative running on the user machine.
projects.audience: Power users and small teams who want AI app generation without hosted builder lock-in.
projects.aiNativeAngle: AI generation is the primary creation surface, while local execution, BYO keys, project files, Electron app packaging and benchmarks make it closer to a product than a prompt demo.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub page showed 20.7k stars, 2.5k forks, 1,526 commits, 227 issues, 9 PRs and latest release v1.4.0 on Jun 17, 2026.
projects.runnability: README advertises Mac/Windows downloads, no signup, BYO AI keys, docs/contributing/security, plus source tree with apps, packages, e2e-tests and Storybook.
projects.recommendedAction: Try the desktop build first; evaluate whether it can replace hosted app builders for small internal tools.

projects.summary: Linux Foundation AAIF open-source local agent with desktop app, CLI, and API for code, research, writing, automation, and data analysis.
projects.audience: Developers and teams that need a cross-provider, cross-extension local agent usable from desktop, terminal and API surfaces.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The agent is the product surface, with 15+ providers, Claude/ChatGPT/Gemini subscription access, 70+ MCP extensions, custom distributions and local workflows.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend AI Agent ranked it #8 with +591 today and 48.1k stars; GitHub showed about 49k stars, 5.2k forks, 4,697 commits, 175 issues, 171 PRs, and latest release v1.37.0 on Jun 3, 2026.
projects.runnability: README provides desktop download, curl-based CLI install, quickstart, installation docs, tutorials, diagnostics, custom distributions and Docker/build files.
projects.recommendedAction: Track as a mature baseline; deep-dive only when release, MCP, or ACP capability changes are material.

projects.summary: Self-hosted dashboard for dispatching AI agent tasks, coordinating multi-agent workflows, tracking spend, managing skills, and auditing security from one SQLite-backed control surface.
projects.audience: Teams running multiple coding/research agents who need task queues, review gates, costs, logs, skills, schedules, and governance in one place.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It treats agent work as an operated system: agents, tasks, skills, memory, MCP calls, evals, trust scores, cron schedules, webhooks, alerts, and quality gates are first-class product objects.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend AI Infrastructure ranked it #4 with +372 stars today; GitHub showed 5.3k stars, 906 forks, 366 commits, 7 issues and 11 PRs, with docs, examples, tests, Docker, OpenAPI and screenshots.
projects.runnability: README documents one-command local install, manual pnpm dev, Docker compose, GHCR image, hardened compose profile, setup page, API examples and optional gateway mode.
projects.recommendedAction: Run the Docker path and test whether Codex/Claude/OpenCode tasks can be registered, queued, reviewed and audited without custom glue.

projects.summary: DeepSeek-native coding agent for terminal and desktop workflows, redesigned around stable prefix-cache economics, long-running sessions, SEARCH/REPLACE review and Go-based native distribution.
projects.audience: Developers using DeepSeek models heavily, especially cost-sensitive users who leave agents running across long coding sessions.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It treats model-cache behavior as a product primitive: the agent loop, command set, session memory, MCP wiring and desktop client are all shaped around keeping prompts cache-stable.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend ranked it #7 in AI Agent on June 9 with +608 stars today; GitTrend repo page showed 13.8k stars, 797 forks and 309 issues, while GitHub showed 747 commits and a main-v2 Go rewrite note.
projects.runnability: Install paths include npm i -g reasonix and brew install esengine/reasonix/reasonix; prebuilt archives and SHA256SUMS are documented for macOS/Linux/Windows.
projects.recommendedAction: Run a small coding benchmark against generic agents using the same DeepSeek key; inspect whether cache metrics and review/apply loop create a real cost/quality advantage.

projects.summary: AI-powered job-search command center that turns Claude Code, Codex, Gemini, Qwen or OpenCode into a pipeline for job evaluation, tailored CV generation, portal scanning, batch processing and tracking.
projects.audience: Job seekers, career switchers and AI builders who want a structured, human-reviewed job-search operating system.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The workflow is agent-native: coding CLIs navigate career pages, reason over CV/job fit, personalize PDFs, update trackers, deduplicate applications and keep a TUI/dashboard view of the pipeline.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend ranked it #6 in AI Agent on June 9 with +754 stars today; GitHub showed 52.1k stars, 10.5k forks, 259 commits, 88 issues and 79 PRs, with demo images and multilingual README.
projects.runnability: Fast path is npx @santifer/career-ops init, then open Claude/Gemini/Codex/Qwen/OpenCode in the generated workspace; manual clone/npm install and Playwright Chromium setup are documented.
projects.recommendedAction: High-priority vertical product study. Run onboarding with a dummy CV and compare its data contract, tracker integrity and HITL safeguards against simpler prompt-pack workflows.

projects.category: Enterprise Browser Agent / Governed Web Automation
projects.summary: Open-source browser agent for enterprise web workflows, translating natural-language tasks into auditable browser actions.
projects.audience: Enterprise automation, QA, operations, and engineering teams that need AI to operate web apps and internal admin systems.
projects.aiNativeAngle: Combines task goals, page understanding, browser actions, human supervision, and governance into an agent workbench.
projects.growthSignal: Surfaced repeatedly in current browser-agent searches with Microsoft organization backing and a distinct enterprise-governance angle.
projects.runnability: Product-shaped public repository; install/demo depth still needs clone-level audit in a follow-up run.
projects.recommendedAction: Run first; benchmark against Skyvern, browser-use, and Playwright MCP on the same enterprise form task.

projects.category: Local Coding Agent / Small-model Agent Runtime
projects.summary: Terminal-native coding agent optimized for local 7B-20B models using budgeted context, forgiving tool parsing, TODO decomposition, patch-first editing, traces, and local memory.
projects.audience: Developers who want private, low-cost, local coding agents instead of relying only on frontier cloud models.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The agent loop is designed around small-model weaknesses: context budgeting, parser repair, tool routing, decomposition, fallback escalation, and observability.
projects.growthSignal: Created 2026-05-18; GitHub API showed 733 stars, 42 forks, and pushed_at 2026-05-19T21:38:44Z.
projects.runnability: README includes npm/npx install, prebuilt binaries, one-line install scripts, env configuration, CLI commands, programmatic API, traces, evals, and model profiles.
projects.recommendedAction: Try on a medium code-fix task and compare local 7B/14B quality against optional cloud fallback.

projects.summary: 终端里的 agentic coding tool，理解代码库、执行常规任务、解释复杂代码并处理 git workflow。
projects.audience: 已在 Claude 生态工作的工程团队、独立开发者、代码审查/维护场景。
projects.aiNativeAngle: 以自然语言驱动 repo 级任务，并包含插件目录、examples、bug report 命令和安装脚本。
projects.growthSignal: GitHub 页面显示约 124k stars、5k+ issues、542 PRs、625 commits；仓库含 demo.gif、plugins、examples。
projects.runnability: README 推荐 curl 安装脚本、Homebrew、Windows install.ps1/WinGet；进入项目目录运行 `claude`。
projects.recommendedAction: 跟 opencode/Codex/Gemini CLI 做同仓库对比；重点评估插件生态和权限/审计体验。

projects.summary: Unified session layer for Claude Code, Codex, Pi, custom agents, policies, sandboxing, and realtime collaboration.
projects.audience: Developers and teams running multiple coding agents or building agent platforms.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The product model is agent sessions, sub-agents, policy enforcement, cloud sandboxes, and live collaboration.
projects.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-11; observed about 1.9k stars, 238 forks, 116 commits, active push on 2026-06-16, docs/examples/SDK/openapi directories.
projects.runnability: README offers install script, uv, pip, Homebrew, direct repo install, and macOS app download.
projects.recommendedAction: Track long term and try local/cloud sandbox orchestration.

projects.summary: Local-first layer that compresses tool outputs, logs, RAG chunks, files and conversation history before they reach an LLM, exposed as library, proxy, wrapper and MCP server.
projects.audience: Heavy Claude/Codex/Cursor/Aider/Copilot users and agent-platform builders paying high context/token costs.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It attacks agent runtime economics directly: reversible context compression, retrieval-on-demand, cross-agent memory and failure mining become part of the agent loop.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend ranked it #3 overall on June 8 with +2.0k stars today and 19.0k stars; GitHub page showed 19.6k stars, 1.3k forks, 1,489 commits, 126 issues and 93 PRs.
projects.runnability: pip/npm/Docker install paths, headroom wrap/proxy/MCP commands, benchmarks, docs, examples and tests are present; no local run audit performed.
projects.recommendedAction: Run a controlled Codex/Claude comparison on the same repo and measure prompt size, retrieval misses and answer quality.

projects.summary: GitHub Agentic Workflows lets teams write natural-language Markdown workflows and run them in GitHub Actions.
projects.audience: Engineering teams automating repository maintenance, issue triage, and CI-side agent tasks.
projects.aiNativeAngle: Treats agent workflows as repository-native artifacts with provider choice and security guardrails.
projects.growthSignal: Visible GitHub page shows about 4.6k stars, 415 forks, 12,531 commits, 192 issues, and 9 PRs.
projects.runnability: Has CLI, install script, docs, actions, Dockerfile, quickstart, and security architecture documentation.
projects.recommendedAction: Create a small repo-maintenance workflow experiment for PR comment or issue triage automation.

projects.summary: Open-source LLM knowledge platform that turns raw documents into RAG Q&A, autonomous reasoning agents, and self-maintaining Wikis.
projects.audience: Enterprise knowledge-base, private RAG, internal Q&A, and IM assistant teams.
projects.aiNativeAngle: Combines ReAct agent, MCP tools, web search, Wiki Mode, graph exploration, IM channels, and Feishu/Notion/Yuque sync.
projects.growthSignal: Visible GitHub page shows about 15.1k stars, 1.9k forks, 1,670 commits, 153 issues, and 30 PRs; v0.5.2 notes include MCP HITL approval and Yuque connector.
projects.runnability: Repository includes frontend, docker, helm, mcp-server, cli, docs, examples, and docker-compose.
projects.recommendedAction: Track and prototype the Yuque/Feishu connector plus agentic wiki flow.

projects.summary: Official GitHub MCP server exposing GitHub repositories, issues, pull requests, and related developer objects to AI agents.
projects.audience: Developers building coding, DevOps, project-management, and repository automation agents.
projects.aiNativeAngle: GitHub becomes an agent-callable action and context plane rather than only a web/API destination.
projects.growthSignal: Official GitHub project and highly relevant in the current MCP/coding-agent ecosystem.
projects.runnability: README documents Docker/binary/configuration paths for running the MCP server.
projects.recommendedAction: Run a minimal agent plus GitHub MCP test: query issues/PRs and generate reviewable actions.

projects.summary: Orchestration layer for parallel coding agents that creates isolated worktrees, branches, PRs, and routes CI/review feedback back to agents.
projects.audience: Engineering teams and platform teams putting Claude Code, Codex, Aider, and similar agents into real software delivery loops.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The product manages a fleet of agents rather than a single assistant, turning planning, execution, feedback repair, and PR lifecycle into an agent-native workflow.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub page snapshot showed about 7.3k stars, 989 forks, 1,274 commits, and hundreds of active issues/PRs; search result was crawled within the last 5 days.
projects.runnability: README provides npm global install, source setup, ao start, and dashboard launch; requires Node, Git, gh CLI, and tmux/ConPTY.
projects.recommendedAction: Run a dry test on a small repository and inspect permissions, GitHub token scope, rollback behavior, and human approval points.

projects.summary: A TypeScript workspace that positions humans, Claude Code, Codex, OpenClaw, Hermes, and other agents as one team in a shared product surface.
projects.audience: Teams experimenting with multi-agent coding/research workflows that need a visible coordination layer rather than isolated terminal sessions.
projects.aiNativeAngle: The product value is the workspace itself: routing work to multiple agents, exposing agent state, and giving humans a common command surface for agent collaboration.
projects.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-22; 311 stars and 29 forks in the API snapshot; pushed 2026-06-23; README includes logo, bilingual docs link, agent badges, Node/PostgreSQL requirements, and hosted homepage.
projects.runnability: README-level audit only. TypeScript app with explicit Node.js 24 and PostgreSQL 16 requirements plus product homepage; no local install/run was performed.
projects.recommendedAction: Priority follow-up: run a minimal local workspace and compare its multi-agent routing with current terminal-based Codex/Claude Code workflows.

projects.summary: CLI and web dashboard for running multiple AI coding agents in isolated worktrees with commits, pushes, PRs, CI watching, and retries.
projects.audience: Engineering teams and solo developers running Claude Code, Cursor CLI, Gemini CLI, or similar coding agents in parallel.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It productizes the execution lifecycle around many agents: isolation, state, audit trail, PR flow, CI feedback, and recovery.
projects.growthSignal: Repository page showed about 203 stars, 31 forks, 1,026 commits, 63 issues, and 33 PRs, with a complete product README and cloud/local paths.
projects.runnability: Provides npx and npm CLI install paths plus a local dashboard at localhost:4050; no clone/run audit performed.
projects.recommendedAction: Run a focused local trial and decompose worktree isolation, agent state machine, CI auto-fix, and PR approval flow.

projects.summary: Secure and elastic infrastructure for running AI-generated code.
projects.audience: Teams building coding agents, automated code execution, previews, and remote dev environments.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It solves the post-generation runtime problem: safely execute, isolate, validate, and lifecycle-manage agent-written code.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub snapshot showed 2,614 commits, 198 releases, and latest v0.183.0 on 2026-05-29.
projects.runnability: Repository includes apps, docker, examples, guides, charts, and infrastructure-oriented product code.
projects.recommendedAction: Add to experiment queue: agent-generated code to Daytona sandbox to execution result.

projects.summary: Open-source neural text-to-speech stack optimized for expressive local speech synthesis and voice-agent use cases.
projects.audience: Voice-agent builders, local AI app developers, audio product teams, and researchers shipping interactive speech UX.
projects.aiNativeAngle: Low-latency expressive voice output is a core interface layer for voice agents, not a secondary media export feature.
projects.growthSignal: Surfaced in a current fastest-growing AI repo roundup with roughly 6.8k stars and about +5.5k weekly growth.
projects.runnability: Repository presents runnable open-source TTS code rather than a paper-only asset; needs local dependency/GPU audit before deeper recommendation.
projects.recommendedAction: Add to voice-agent watchlist and test minimal synthesis path; compare latency and voice quality against hosted TTS APIs.

projects.summary: 把复杂软件和服务包装成 agent 可用的 CLI harness/skill，让 agent 操作外部软件。
projects.audience: 需要让 coding/automation agent 稳定调用图形软件、专业工具和网站的工程团队。
projects.aiNativeAngle: 目标不是给人写 CLI，而是把所有软件 agent-ready 作为运行层抽象。
projects.growthSignal: GitHub 页面显示约 36.6k stars、3.6k forks、640 commits、38 issues、22 PRs；README 新闻区显示 2026-04 中旬仍在持续合并 harness。
projects.runnability: README 提供 CLI-Hub、demos、tests、`npx skills add HKUDS/CLI-Anything --skill <skill-name>` 形式安装；仓库含大量 agent-harness 与 skills。
projects.recommendedAction: 放入“agent tool surface”横评；优先验证 2-3 个真实软件 harness 是否稳定可复现。

projects.category: AI Coding Project Director / Governance Rail
projects.summary: A Rust local CLI/TUI that drives already-logged-in Claude Code, Codex or OpenCode through a nine-phase governed delivery pipeline from clarification to proof pack.
projects.audience: Builders using coding agents for full project delivery who need PRD, architecture, UI/UX review, implementation gates, QA evidence and delivery artifacts.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It wraps agent coding with deterministic process state, gates, audit traces, knowledge/rules and proof packs, treating agent output as something that needs operational governance.
projects.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-19; 75 stars, 29 forks; pushed 2026-06-22; README is bilingual and detailed; repo has Cargo workspace, npm distribution, docs, spec, tests, knowledge and website.
projects.runnability: README documents `npm install -g umadev`, `umadev init`, `umadev`; source build via Cargo is documented. This run did not install or launch it.
projects.recommendedAction: High-priority trial on a small empty app: test whether the stage gates produce useful artifacts or mainly add ceremony.

projects.summary: A local Tauri + React MCP gateway that lets multiple AI coding tools share one managed MCP server catalog, profile scope, auth, audit log, and lazy tool discovery surface.
projects.audience: Developers and small teams using Claude, Cursor, VS Code, Codex, and other AI tools with overlapping MCP server setups.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It treats MCP sprawl as an agent-operating problem: collapse many server tools into a small searchable meta-tool surface, centralize credentials in the OS keychain, and scope tool access per agent profile.
projects.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-19; 5 stars, 1 fork; pushed 2026-06-21; README includes screenshots, demo GIF, governance claims, Tauri app, gateway binary, docs, scripts, package files, and src-tauri/source tree.
projects.runnability: README describes desktop app + conduit-gateway architecture. Root contains package.json, src, src-tauri, docs, scripts, screenshots, and Vite/Tauri config. This run did not build or launch it.
projects.recommendedAction: High-priority local trial: install/build, connect two real MCP servers, then test lazy discovery, per-client profile scoping, destructive-tool hiding, and audit logs.

projects.skipReason: Mature workflow automation baseline; active but not a fresh daily discovery for this radar.

projects.category: Claude Code Model Router / Coding Agent Infrastructure
projects.summary: Routes Claude Code requests to different providers/models with transformers, logging, dynamic model switching and GitHub Actions support.
projects.audience: Developers already using Claude Code who want cost, latency, long-context, local-model, or CI routing control.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It treats coding-agent traffic as an infrastructure plane: route by task type, subagent, provider, environment and workflow instead of binding one agent UI to one model endpoint.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub README is deep and operational, covering model routing, multi-provider support, transformations, CLI model management, subagent routing and CI integration.
projects.runnability: Install path is explicit: install Claude Code, then `npm install -g @musistudio/claude-code-router`, configure `~/.claude-code-router/config.json`, and run via `ccr`/activated env vars.
projects.recommendedAction: Run a small cost-routing experiment: Sonnet for planning, cheaper/local model for background or repetitive tasks.

projects.summary: macOS menu bar app that generates, builds, runs, edits, and exports small native SwiftUI apps from prompts.
projects.audience: Mac power users and developers who want personal utilities generated by AI.
projects.aiNativeAngle: LLM drives the full prompt-to-SwiftUI-app lifecycle with local and hosted model support.
projects.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-14; GitHub web observed 133 stars, 7 forks, 2 releases, v0.1.1 on 2026-06-15.
projects.runnability: Releases/website downloads, xcode-select setup, script/build.sh run, script/test.sh, screenshots and example prompts.
projects.recommendedAction: Track as a prompt-to-local-app pattern and evaluate small-tool generation.

projects.summary: Local-first Claude Design alternative with native desktop app, prototypes, slides, images, videos, HyperFrames, HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export, CLI and MCP integration.
projects.audience: Product, design, and development teams that want design-system-shaped artifacts inside agent workflows.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It frames design delivery as agent-consumable skills, design systems, automations, plugins, MCP server calls, and sandboxed artifact preview.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend AI Agent ranked it #5 with +774 today and 61.7k stars; GitHub README shows product tour, core page screenshots, Studio artifact screenshots, and MCP install matrix.
projects.runnability: README shows native app, CLI/MCP, sandbox preview, export capabilities and multi-agent support; no clone/run audit was performed.
projects.recommendedAction: Add to replication candidates and first deconstruct the design-system-to-artifact-to-export workflow instead of copying the full desktop app.

projects.summary: Installs, configures, routes, and health-checks internet-reading capabilities for AI agents across web, YouTube, RSS, GitHub, X, Bilibili, Xiaohongshu, Reddit, LinkedIn and other channels.
projects.audience: Individuals and teams that need agents to perform real external research, social listening, video/web/RSS/GitHub reading, and channel-specific retrieval.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It turns external internet access into an agent capability layer with channel routing, fallback backends, doctor diagnostics, installation flows, and skill registration.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend AI Agent ranked it #3 with +1.1k today and 24.3k stars; GitHub showed 26.5k stars, 2.2k forks, 282 commits, 27 issues, 14 PRs, docs, tests, install/update docs, and safe mode.
projects.runnability: README documents one-line agent install/update, safe mode, pip install, agent-reach doctor, channel configuration, dry-run, uninstall and security guidance; no local install audit was performed.
projects.recommendedAction: Try safe install in an isolated environment and validate web, YouTube, RSS, and GitHub zero-config channels as a future radar source.

projects.summary: AI agent skill that searches Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, GitHub and web sources, scores signals by real engagement, then synthesizes a grounded last-30-days brief.
projects.audience: Operators, researchers, sales/product people and agent users who need recent community truth rather than stale web search summaries.
projects.aiNativeAngle: It uses an agent as a cross-platform research engine with source-specific connectors, entity resolution, competitive comparison, HTML brief export and agent-judged synthesis.
projects.growthSignal: GitTrend AI Skills ranked it #1 with +3.5k stars today; GitHub showed 39.3k stars, 3.2k forks, 623 commits, 75 issues and 72 PRs.
projects.runnability: README documents Claude plugin install, npx skills add for Codex/Cursor/Copilot/Gemini-style hosts, zero-config sources for Reddit/HN/Polymarket/GitHub, and guided setup for X/YouTube/TikTok.
projects.recommendedAction: Use it as a radar-source experiment on one topic and compare output quality, citation fidelity and auth friction against this current manual workflow.

projects.summary: MCP-style documentation context provider for coding agents.
projects.audience: Coding-agent and developer-tool builders needing fresh library docs in prompts.
projects.aiNativeAngle: Improves agent output by providing current docs/context through MCP-like workflow.
projects.growthSignal: GitHub topic page showed about 26.8k stars, 1.4k forks, and an update on 2026-06-07.

esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix.summary: DeepSeek-native coding agent for terminal and desktop workflows, redesigned around stable prefix-cache economics, long-running sessions, SEARCH/REPLACE review and Go-based native distribution.
esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix.audience: Developers using DeepSeek models heavily, especially cost-sensitive users who leave agents running across long coding sessions.
esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix.aiNativeAngle: It treats model-cache behavior as a product primitive: the agent loop, command set, session memory, MCP wiring and desktop client are all shaped around keeping prompts cache-stable.
esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix.growthSignal: GitTrend ranked it #7 in AI Agent on June 9 with +608 stars today; GitTrend repo page showed 13.8k stars, 797 forks and 309 issues, while GitHub showed 747 commits and a main-v2 Go rewrite note.
esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix.runnability: Install paths include npm i -g reasonix and brew install esengine/reasonix/reasonix; prebuilt archives and SHA256SUMS are documented for macOS/Linux/Windows.
esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix.recommendedAction: Run a small coding benchmark against generic agents using the same DeepSeek key; inspect whether cache metrics and review/apply loop create a real cost/quality advantage.

umacloud/umadev.aiNativeAngle: It wraps agent coding with deterministic process state, gates, audit traces, knowledge/rules and proof packs, treating agent output as something that needs operational governance.

umacloud/umadev.runnability: README documents `npm install -g umadev`, `umadev init`, `umadev`; source build via Cargo is documented. This run did not install or launch it.
umacloud/umadev.recommendedAction: High-priority trial on a small empty app: test whether the stage gates produce useful artifacts or mainly add ceremony.

tsouth89/conduit.summary: A local Tauri + React MCP gateway that lets multiple AI coding tools share one managed MCP server catalog, profile scope, auth, audit log, and lazy tool discovery surface.
tsouth89/conduit.audience: Developers and small teams using Claude, Cursor, VS Code, Codex, and other AI tools with overlapping MCP server setups.
tsouth89/conduit.aiNativeAngle: It treats MCP sprawl as an agent-operating problem: collapse many server tools into a small searchable meta-tool surface, centralize credentials in the OS keychain, and scope tool access per agent profile.
tsouth89/conduit.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-19; 5 stars, 1 fork; pushed 2026-06-21; README includes screenshots, demo GIF, governance claims, Tauri app, gateway binary, docs, scripts, package files, and src-tauri/source tree.
tsouth89/conduit.runnability: README describes desktop app + conduit-gateway architecture. Root contains package.json, src, src-tauri, docs, scripts, screenshots, and Vite/Tauri config. This run did not build or launch it.
tsouth89/conduit.recommendedAction: High-priority local trial: install/build, connect two real MCP servers, then test lazy discovery, per-client profile scoping, destructive-tool hiding, and audit logs.

musistudio/claude-code-router.category: Claude Code Model Router / Coding Agent Infrastructure
musistudio/claude-code-router.summary: Routes Claude Code requests to different providers/models with transformers, logging, dynamic model switching and GitHub Actions support.

musistudio/claude-code-router.aiNativeAngle: It treats coding-agent traffic as an infrastructure plane: route by task type, subagent, provider, environment and workflow instead of binding one agent UI to one model endpoint.

musistudio/claude-code-router.runnability: Install path is explicit: install Claude Code, then `npm install -g @musistudio/claude-code-router`, configure `~/.claude-code-router/config.json`, and run via `ccr`/activated env vars.
musistudio/claude-code-router.recommendedAction: Run a small cost-routing experiment: Sonnet for planning, cheaper/local model for background or repetitive tasks.

sricola/drydock.category: Coding Agent Sandbox / macOS VM Isolation
sricola/drydock.summary: Runs Claude Code or OpenAI Codex against a disposable macOS VM copy of a repo and returns only an approved git diff.
sricola/drydock.audience: Developers and teams that want autonomous coding-agent runs without exposing real API keys, network egress, push credentials, or the host filesystem.
sricola/drydock.aiNativeAngle: Treats coding agents as untrusted executors and moves the product value to containment, short-lived budget-capped tokens, network allowlists, and diff-only approval.
sricola/drydock.growthSignal: Created 2026-06-15; 6 stars; pushed 2026-06-23; README has threat model, demo breach GIF, release badge, docs, examples, tests, Go modules, Makefile, and dedicated SECURITY/THREAT_MODEL files.
sricola/drydock.runnability: macOS 26+ Apple Silicon oriented; repo includes cmd/config/demo/docs/examples/image/internal/tests and Go build structure. No local VM run performed.
sricola/drydock.recommendedAction: High-priority experiment: run one disposable Codex task, verify blocked egress/key isolation, and compare its approval loop with existing permission-prompt workflows.

fujibee/agmsg.summary: A Bash + SQLite message layer that lets Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Copilot CLI, OpenCode and similar CLI agents talk to each other without a daemon.

fujibee/agmsg.aiNativeAngle: It treats multi-agent collaboration as a local durable communication primitive, with shared rooms, history replay, monitor/turn delivery modes and agent-specific skill/plugin entries.
fujibee/agmsg.growthSignal: Created 2026-04-02; 792 stars, 56 forks; pushed 2026-06-22; Product Hunt badge; README has demo GIF/screenshots, npm/npx/plugin installs, architecture/privacy docs, tests and scripts.
fujibee/agmsg.runnability: Requires bash and sqlite3; setup script, clone install, npm/npx and Claude Code plugin marketplace paths documented. Not installed in this run.
fujibee/agmsg.recommendedAction: Run a two-agent Codex/Claude review handoff in a disposable repo; check whether message history improves handoff quality versus a shared Markdown file.
